prs.title: Feat: Support DeepseekV4-Pro on MI355 Platform
prs.author: bobofang11235
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Main changes include:
- Add ROCm sparse MLA fallback coverage for DeepSeek-V4 prefill/decode.
- Add ROCm sparse attention indexer fallback registration.
- Preserve the corrected `quantize_and_insert_k_cache` UE8M0 block-layout writer for SWA KV cache insertion.
- Add ROCm-safe fallback handling for DeepSeek-V4 `wo_a` FP8 einsum.
- Keep unstable optimization paths env-gated, including Sparse MLA BMM and `wo_a` BMM prototypes.
- Validate the no-eager piecewise CUDAGraph serving path on ROCm.

## ENV
aiter version: dcb0639d870783c2bc0c530e465f301032e756dc
flydsl version: f85bd3f7de80295370deae891e27fc9a34782806

## Test Plan
Server command used for the best validated accuracy run:

```
export PYTHONPATH=/opt/aiter:/opt/aiter/aiter/jit/utils
export HIP_FORCE_DEV_KERNARG=1
export HSA_NO_SCRATCH_RECLAIM=1
export NCCL_MIN_NCHANNELS=112
export TORCH_BLAS_PREFER_HIPBLASLT=1
export AITER_LOG_LEVEL=WARNING
export VLLM_NO_USAGE_STATS=1
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_LINEAR=0
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_RMSNORM=1
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_TRITON_ROPE=1
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_MOE=1
export VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_MLA=1
export VLLM_DSV4_ROCM_WOA_BMM=1
vllm serve /dev/shm/models/DeepSeek-V4-Pro \
  --trust-remote-code \
  --tokenizer-mode deepseek_v4 \
  --reasoning-parser deepseek_v4 \
  --tool-call-parser deepseek_v4 \
  --enable-auto-tool-choice \
  --tensor-parallel-size 8 \
  --quantization fp8 \
  --max-model-len 32768 \
  --max-num-seqs 32 \
  --max-num-batched-tokens 8192 \
  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.85 \
  --disable-custom-all-reduce \
  --host 0.0.0.0 \
  --port 8000 \
  --kv-cache-dtype fp8_e4m3 \
  --compilation-config '{"mode":3,"cudagraph_mode":1,"cudagraph_capture_sizes":[1,2,4,8]}'
```

Accuracy test:
```
lm_eval run \
  --model local-completions \
  --model_args model=/dev/shm/models/DeepSeek-V4-Pro,base_url=http://127.0.0.1:8000/v1/completions,num_concurrent=2,tokenizer_back

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix GPT-OSS Quark MXFP4+FP8 MoE startup
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Fix GPT-OSS Quark MXFP4+FP8 MoE startup on ROCm/gfx950 by applying the padding required by the Triton/CDNA4 MXFP4 scale layout, and align the GPT-OSS Quark monolithic MoE method with the current `MoERunner` call signature. The GPQA ROCm config for `amd/gpt-oss-20b-MoE-Quant-W-MXFP4-A-FP8-KV-FP8` failed during model startup while processing MoE weights:

```text
RuntimeError: shape '[-1, 90, 2, 16, 11, 2, 4, 1]' is invalid for input of size 8294400
```

The failing path is `QuarkOCP_MX_MoEMethod_OSS`, used for GPT-OSS Quark weights with MXFP4 weights and static FP8 activations. This subclass still swizzles MXFP4 weights and scales through Triton kernels in `process_weights_after_loading()`.

However, the base `QuarkOCP_MX_MoEMethod` treats `w_mxfp4_a_fp8` as an emulation path for sizing purposes, so it does not apply the MXFP4 backend padding. On ROCm/CDNA4, Triton scale swizzling requires aligned dimensions. For the 20B TP=2 case, the unpadded dimensions produce scale shapes based on `hidden_size=2880` and `intermediate_size_per_partition=1440`; `hidden_size / 32 = 90`, which is not compatible with the CDNA4 scale swizzle layout.

## Testing

Ran the focused padded MoE test file:

```text
pytest -s -v evals/gpt_oss/test_gpqa_correctness.py --config-list-file=configs/models-gfx950.txt
```

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm] ROCm DeepEP API updated to latest
prs.author: itej89
prs.body_head:
Enablement of all the DeepEP API parameters

## Purpose
This PR is to match ROCm DeepEP API with the vllm 

co-authored by : @lcskrishna 

The following changes are preformed

Code
- DeepEP buffer initialization arguments
- DeepEP dispatch call arguments support for low latency

ROCm Docker test stage
- Updates to the DeepEP and ROCShmem Commit IDs 
- Installation of rdma-core v62 required for latest ROCShmem

## Test Plan
**STEP1:** Build ROCm VLLM Image
```
docker build -f ./docker/Dockerfile.rocm_base -t  <IMG_VLLM_ROCM_BASE>  . 

DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build --no-cache  --target test --progress=plain -t  <FINAL_VLLM_IMAGE_NAME>  -f ./docker/Dockerfile.rocm  --build-
arg BASE_IMAGE=<IMG_VLLM_ROCM_BASE>   .

```
**STEP 2:** Please use below command to run VLLM with DeepEP on single node. Select _--all2all-backend_  as either  _deepep_low_latency_ or _deepep_high_throughput_.
```
docker run -it --rm \
  --device /dev/dri \
  --device /dev/kfd \
  --privileged \
  --device /dev/infiniband \
  -v /dev/infiniband:/dev/infiniband \
  --network host \
  --ipc host \
  --group-add video \
  --cap-add SYS_PTRACE \
  --security-opt seccomp=unconfined \
  --ulimit memlock=-1 \
  --shm-size 64G \
  -v <HOST_DS3_MODEL_PATH>:/models/DeepSeek-V3\
  -e HSA_NO_SCRATCH_RECLAIM=1 \
  -e ROCSHMEM_HEAP_SIZE=7524589824 \
  -e VLLM_ENGINE_READY_TIMEOUT_S=7800 \
  --name <CONTAINER_NAME> \
  --entrypoint vllm \
 <FINAL_VLLM_IMAGE_NAME> \
  serve /models/DeepSeek-V3 \
    --tensor-parallel-size 1 \
    --data-parallel-size 8 \
    --enforce-eager \
    --enable-expert-parallel \
    --gpu-memory-utilization 0.8 \
    --host 127.0.0.1 \
    --port 2000 \
    --all2all-backend   deepep_low_latency | deepep_high_throughput
```
**STEP 3:** Please use below command to run VLLM with DeepEP on two nodes. Select _--all2all-backend_  as either  _deepep_low_latency_ or _deepep_high_throughput_.
```
# On Node 1
docker run -it --rm \

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Align spec decode logprob test prefill settings
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
This PR fixes a ROCm-only failure in `test_spec_decode_logprobs` for the ngram speculative decoding cases. The test compares logprobs from a regular base-model run against logprobs from a speculative decoding run. For that comparison to isolate speculative decoding, both LLM instances need to use the same execution settings. Before this change, the spec-decode LLM forced chunked prefill with `enable_chunked_prefill=True` and `max_num_batched_tokens=32`, while the reference LLM did not.

On ROCm, floating-point reductions can vary with batch geometry and scheduling. That means the two runs could produce slightly different logprobs even when speculative decoding itself was behaving correctly. The observed failure was a logprob difference of about `0.235`, which exceeded the test tolerance.

This change factors the shared LLM options into `llm_kwargs` and passes them to both the reference and spec-decode LLMs. The only intended difference between the two runs is now the speculative decoding configuration, so the test better serves its original purpose.

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm][AITER] Enable shuffled rope and KV-cache fusion
prs.author: tuukkjs
prs.body_head:
## Summary
- enable the `ROCM_AITER_FA` fused rope/KV-cache update path when `VLLM_ROCM_SHUFFLE_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT=1` by reshaping shuffled KV caches before `triton_rope_and_cache`
- keep FP8 scale application aligned with the existing shuffled cache representation and add compile-pass coverage for the shuffled ROCm AITER FA path
- on `amd/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-FP8-KV`, the fusion improved output throughput at all 9 tested benchmark points vs both baselines up to `+8.63%` vs `main_shuffle0` (main branch `VLLM_ROCM_SHUFFLE_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT=0`) and `+3.88%` vs `main_shuffle1` (main branch `VLLM_ROCM_SHUFFLE_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT=1`)
- requires AITER support for shuffled 5D `value_cache`, introduced in `4491d152fd14ac5cae407c48cbb2ebb975e4ba69`, or later

AI assistance was used for this PR.

## Test plan
- Ran `lm_eval` correctness checks on `amd/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-FP8-KV` for `main_shuffle0`, `main_shuffle1`, and `pr_shuffle1`
- Ran perf benchmarks for the same three scenarios.
- The exercised server configs correspond to `vllm serve` invocations like:
```bash
VLLM_ROCM_QUICK_REDUCE_QUANTIZATION=INT4 \
VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 \
VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_MHA=1 \
VLLM_ROCM_SHUFFLE_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT=0/1 \
vllm serve amd/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-FP8-KV \
  --async-scheduling \
  --attention-backend ROCM_AITER_FA \
  --compilation-config '{"custom_ops":["-rms_norm","-quant_fp8","-silu_and_mul"],"cudagraph_mode":"FULL_AND_PIECEWISE","use_inductor_graph_partition":true,"pass_config":{"fuse_rope_kvcache":true}}' \
  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.94 \
  --host 0.0.0.0 \
  --kv-cache-dtype fp8 \
  --max-model-len 10240 \
  --max-num-batched-tokens 32768 \
  --max-num-seqs 256 \
  --no-enable-prefix-caching \
  --tensor-parallel-size 1
```
- The patched shuffled runs used image <...> together with a bind-mounted `rocm_aiter_fa.py` containing this change.

## Test results

### `lm_eval` (`gsm8k`)
| Scenario | Batch size | Flexible exact match | Strict exact match |

prs.title: [CI][AMD][BugFix] Prevent triton compiler error when running test_moe_layer with use_ep = True on ROCm
prs.author: rasmith
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
When running `test_moe_layer`, the following Triton compiler error is frequently encountered:

```
triton.compiler.errors.CompilationError: at 177:22:
        # channel-wise
        elif per_channel_quant:
            b_scale_ptrs = (
                b_scale_ptr + off_experts * stride_bse + offs_bn[None, :] * stride_bsn
            )
            b_scale = tl.load(b_scale_ptrs)
            # Load per-token scale for activations
            a_scale_ptrs = a_scale_ptr + (offs_token // top_k) * stride_asm
            a_scale = tl.load(a_scale_ptrs, mask=token_mask, other=0.0)[:, None]
        # tensor-wise
        else:
            a_scale = tl.load(a_scale_ptr)
                      ^
'constexpr_type' object has no attribute 'is_ptr'
```
This happens because `process_fp8_input_tensor_strategy_moe `processes the incoming scales using the following [line](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/e54894fc85a9861fb38a49701b5844462c3d77e4/vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/utils/fp8_utils.py#L1146):

`return w13_input_scale.max(), w2_input_scale.max()   ` 

Which will always return tensors with `ndim == 0`.  For example,  

```
>>> import torch
>>> t=torch.rand((1,2,3,4),device="cuda")
>>> t.max()
tensor(0.9932, device='cuda:0')
>>> t.max().ndim
0
```

When `use_ep = True`,  the following [line ](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/prepare_finalize/naive_dp_ep.py#L48) in `naive_dp_ep.py`

```
skip_gather_scales = a1q_scale is None or a1q_scale.ndim == 0 
scales = None if skip_gather_scales else [a1q_scale]
```
causes the `scales `return value to become `None`, which arrives [here ](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/blob/main/vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/modular_kernel.py#L1372) in `moduler_kernel.py`.

When this `None` scale is passed into `invoke_fused_moe_triton_kernel`, the `None` value is passed into the `fused_moe_kernel` [here](https://github.com/vl

prs.title: [ROCm][perf] Use workspace manager for sparse indexer allocations
prs.author: tuukkjs
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Replace dynamic per-call allocations in the ROCm sparse attention indexer with workspace manager allocations.
This eliminates caching allocator bloat that caused OOM crashes under sustained MTP load.

With MTP speculative decoding enabled on ROCm, the sparse attention indexer dynamically allocates temporary buffers
(`k_fp8`, `k_scale` per prefill chunk; `out_qk` per decode call) on every invocation.
These varying-size allocations cause PyTorch's caching allocator to retain large amounts of unused memory (reserved >> allocated), eventually exhausting GPU memory available to the HIP runtime for kernel scratch space.
This manifests as non-deterministic memory access faults or `HSA_STATUS_ERROR_OUT_OF_RESOURCES` errors.

This is a duplicate of stale PR #38704 by @gronsti-amd (who is away at the moment), rebased onto current main. AI assistance was used for this PR.

## Solution

- **Prefill path:** Replace per-chunk `torch.empty` for k_fp8/k_scale with a single `workspace_manager.get_simultaneous()` call, slicing per chunk. Matches the existing CUDA path in `sparse_attn_indexer.py`.
- **Decode path:** Replace `torch.full` for `out_qk` in `rocm_fp8_paged_mqa_logits` with a workspace manager view filled with `-inf`.
- **Memory profiling path:** Reserve workspace for both prefill and decode buffer sizes in the real impl's early-exit path (not the fake impl, which runs under FakeTensor mode during `torch.compile`).

## Test Plan

**Environment:**
- Image: `vllm/vllm-openai-rocm:nightly-7b1bc0a3eb01a6bc2650eda9970049f7825240d7`
- aiter: `amd-aiter 0.1.10.post3`
- Model: DeepSeek-V3.2, TP=8, 8x MI355X

**Three experiments were run for validation:**

1. **No MTP (baseline):** Verifies the workspace manager changes do not regress the non-speculative decoding path.
2. **MTP=1 (1 speculative token):** Validates workspace-managed allocations under MTP with a single draft token.
3. **MTP=2 (2 speculative tokens):** The configuration that pre

prs.title: [ROCm][Bugfix][GPTOSS]: fix input_ids and expert_map args for quark w4a8 gptoss
prs.author: Rohan138

prs.title: [Bugfix][CI][Hardware][AMD] Fix various e4m3fn -> e4m3fnuz normalization issues
prs.author: mawong-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
This PR fixes various issues in e4m3fnuz handling encountered by various quantization code paths (ModelOpt, online). In particular, on `e4m3fnuz` platforms, `normalize_e4m3fn_to_e4m3fnuz` should be called exactly once on a given FP8 linear layer after weights are loaded.
However,
- Some PyTorch linear kernel-based code paths used during ModelOpt quantization were not calling this function.
- Some code paths used in online quantization methods were trying to call this function twice, once in the linear layer and once more in the linear layer's kernel implementation.

The end result is that the Quantization test group should now be passing on ROCm.

This PR also removes a Quark mxfp4 model that was causing failures in the Kernels MoE test group because it is incompatible with HF Transformers v5.
## Test Plan
`VLLM_TEST_FORCE_LOAD_FORMAT=auto pytest -v -s quantization/ --ignore quantization/test_blackwell_moe.py`
`pytest -v -s kernels/moe --ignore=kernels/moe/test_modular_oai_triton_moe.py`
These tests should be run as part of the Quantization and Kernels MoE test groups in vLLM AMD CI.

## Test Result
The above test groups pass, and there are no regressions.

cc @kenroche and @AndreasKaratzas

---
